---
Title: Scale Domain Analysis
Author: Isabelle LT
Created: 2024-11-27
Inputs: ../Data/scale_of_effect_final_models.csv
Outputs: HGAM model of scale relationships, first derivative plots identifying domain transitions
Description: Uses a hierarchical GAM (HGAM) to model how the optimal landscape extent varies with response extent across landscape configuration metrics. First derivatives with simulation-based confidence intervals are used to identify domain transitions where the scale relationship changes.
---

```{R Load environment}
library(mvtnorm)
library(MuMIn)
library(car)
library(corrplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(cowplot)
library(mgcv)
library(glue)
library(segmented)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(gratia)
library(marginaleffects)
```

```{R Load data}
# Scale of effect results from 1_scale_of_effects analysis
model_table <- read_csv("../Data/scale_of_effect_final_models.csv")
```

```{R HGAM}
# Filter to only additive models (excluding interaction models)
# and create the necessary dataframes
edge_df <- model_table %>%
  filter(metric == "edge", type == "add") %>%
  dplyr::select(response_area_ha, metric_extent_ha) %>%
  rename(
    response_extent = response_area_ha,
    landscape_extent = metric_extent_ha
  ) %>%
  mutate(landscape_metric = "Edge density")

pafrac_df <- model_table %>%
  filter(metric == "pafrac", type == "add") %>%
  dplyr::select(response_area_ha, metric_extent_ha) %>%
  rename(
    response_extent = response_area_ha,
    landscape_extent = metric_extent_ha
  ) %>%
  mutate(landscape_metric = "PAFRAC")

contag_df <- model_table %>%
  filter(metric == "contag", type == "add") %>%
  dplyr::select(response_area_ha, metric_extent_ha) %>%
  rename(
    response_extent = response_area_ha,
    landscape_extent = metric_extent_ha
  ) %>%
  mutate(landscape_metric = "CONTAG")

cai_cv_df <- model_table %>%
  filter(metric == "cai_cv", type == "add") %>%
  dplyr::select(response_area_ha, metric_extent_ha) %>%
  rename(
    response_extent = response_area_ha,
    landscape_extent = metric_extent_ha
  ) %>%
  mutate(landscape_metric = "CAI CV")

shdi_df <- model_table %>%
  filter(metric == "shdi", type == "add") %>%
  dplyr::select(response_area_ha, metric_extent_ha) %>%
  rename(
    response_extent = response_area_ha,
    landscape_extent = metric_extent_ha
  ) %>%
  mutate(landscape_metric = "Shannon diversity")

# Combine all dataframes into a single long format dataframe
long_df <- bind_rows(edge_df, pafrac_df, contag_df, cai_cv_df, shdi_df) %>%
  dplyr::select(landscape_extent, response_extent, landscape_metric)

long_df$landscape_metric <- as.factor(long_df$landscape_metric)
hgam_model <- gam(
  landscape_extent ~ s(response_extent, by = landscape_metric, bs = "fs", m = 2, k = 4) +
    s(landscape_metric, bs = "re"),
  data = long_df, method = "REML"
)

domain_lines <- data.frame(
  landscape_metric = c("CAI CV", "CONTAG", "PAFRAC", "Shannon diversity"),
  xintercept = c(14, 11, 13, 13)
)

model_I <- marginaleffects::plot_predictions(hgam_model,
  condition = c("response_extent", "landscape_metric", "landscape_metric"),
  points = 0.5,
  rug = FALSE
) +
  geom_vline(
    data = domain_lines,
    aes(xintercept = xintercept),
    linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Response extent (ha)",
    y = "Configuration metric landscape extent (ha)"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```


```{R Derivatives for inflection points}
# Quantify inflection points using derivatives
metric_name <- "Edge density"
subset_data <- long_df %>% filter(landscape_metric == metric_name)

# Create prediction dataset
newdat <- data.frame(
  response_extent = seq(min(subset_data$response_extent),
    max(subset_data$response_extent),
    length.out = 200
  ),
  landscape_metric = metric_name
)

# Create datasets shifted by epsilon for numerical differentiation
eps <- 1 # Step size is 1 ha to anchor in spatial ecology realism
newdat_p_eps <- newdat %>% mutate(response_extent = response_extent + eps)
newdat_m_eps <- newdat %>% mutate(response_extent = response_extent - eps)

# Get linear predictor matrices
sim_lp <- predict(hgam_model, newdata = newdat, type = "lpmatrix")
sim_lp_p <- predict(hgam_model, newdata = newdat_p_eps, type = "lpmatrix")
sim_lp_m <- predict(hgam_model, newdata = newdat_m_eps, type = "lpmatrix")

# Generate coefficient simulations
n_sim <- 1000
set.seed(42)
sim_coef <- rmvnorm(n_sim, coef(hgam_model), vcov(hgam_model, unconditional = TRUE))

# Calculate predictions for each simulation
pred_original <- sim_lp %*% t(sim_coef)
pred_p_eps <- sim_lp_p %*% t(sim_coef)
pred_m_eps <- sim_lp_m %*% t(sim_coef)

# Calculate derivatives
first_derivative <- (pred_p_eps - pred_m_eps) / (2 * eps)
second_derivative <- (pred_p_eps - 2 * pred_original + pred_m_eps) / (eps^2)

# Summarize across simulations
newdat$first_deriv_median <- apply(first_derivative, 1, median)
newdat$first_deriv_lower <- apply(first_derivative, 1, quantile, 0.025)
newdat$first_deriv_upper <- apply(first_derivative, 1, quantile, 0.975)
newdat$second_deriv_median <- apply(second_derivative, 1, median)
newdat$second_deriv_lower <- apply(second_derivative, 1, quantile, 0.025)
newdat$second_deriv_upper <- apply(second_derivative, 1, quantile, 0.975)

# Identify where first derivative CI excludes zero on both sides of a boundary
domain_boundaries <- newdat %>%
  arrange(response_extent) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_positive = first_deriv_lower > 0,
    sig_negative = first_deriv_upper < 0,
    not_sig = first_deriv_lower <= 0 & first_deriv_upper >= 0,
    domain_change = sig_positive != lag(sig_positive) |
      sig_negative != lag(sig_negative) |
      not_sig != lag(not_sig)
  ) %>%
  filter(domain_change == TRUE)

# Domain transition values (ha) for each metric
domain_transitions <- c(
  "CAI CV" = 14,
  "CONTAG" = 11,
  "PAFRAC" = 13,
  "Shannon diversity" = 13
)

# Plot first derivative
first_der_plot <- ggplot(newdat, aes(x = response_extent)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = first_deriv_lower, ymax = first_deriv_upper), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = first_deriv_median)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  {
    if (metric_name %in% names(domain_transitions)) geom_vline(xintercept = domain_transitions[[metric_name]], linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 1.2)
  } +
  labs(
    x = "Response extent (ha)",
    y = glue(metric_name, " first derivative")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18)
  )
```
